purpose: Describes Synapse's overflow queue at {tracker_root}/queue/ that holds swarm plans when all active dashboards are occupied. Each queue item mirrors dashboard structure (initialization.json + logs.json + progress/) so promotion is a directory copy. Documents how items enter the queue (when selectDashboard finds none free), queue read operations (listQueue, readQueueInit/Progress/Logs), and the promotion-to-dashboard process.
gotchas: Queue items have no live workers running -- progress/ stays empty until promoted to a dashboard
Queue is unlimited in size; dashboards have no fixed slot count either (despite legacy 'slots 1-5' wording in some places)
Promotion requires archiving the target dashboard first if it has previous data
patterns: Same-shape directory structure between queue items and dashboards (enables copy-based promotion)
conventions: Queue subdirectories named queue1, queue2, ... in insertion order
QueueService is the canonical reader; promotion handled by master/orchestrator logic
